prompt: Explain in two short sentences why CPU interference can increase inference latency on an embedded board.
reference_for_bertscore: CPU interference can increase inference latency because the model shares cores, caches and memory bandwidth with other processes. This contention delays prompt processing and token generation.

prompt: Explain the difference between throughput and latency in AI inference using one short paragraph.
reference_for_bertscore: Latency measures how long one inference takes from input to output, while throughput measures how many tokens or requests are processed per unit of time. A system can have high throughput but still be unsuitable for real-time use if individual responses arrive too late.
output: Throughput refers to the number of tasks or data samples processed in a given time period, typically measured in units such as "per second" or "per minute". It represents the rate at which data is processed by the AI system, indicating how many data samples can be processed per second or per minute. Latency, on the other hand, is the time required to complete a task or data sample, measured in units such as "seconds" or "minutes". Latency reflects the time it takes for the data to be processed by the AI system, which can be influenced by factors such as network latency and the size of the data being processed. Throughput is more relevant for performance measures, while latency is important for efficiency in terms of processing time.

prompt: Explain the difference between throughput and latency in AI inference using one short paragraph.
reference_for_bertscore: Latency measures how long one inference takes from input to output, while throughput measures how many tokens or requests are processed per unit of time. A system can have high throughput but still be unsuitable for real-time use if individual responses arrive too late.
output: Throughput refers to the number of tasks that can be executed per unit of time, typically measured in megaflops (Mflops) or teraflops (Tflops). It represents the rate at which computations can be performed, with more throughput means more tasks can be executed in the same amount of time. Latency, on the other hand, refers to the time it takes for a task to complete. In AI inference, latency is the time it takes for the AI to process and deliver the results, which is measured in milliseconds (ms) or microseconds (us). High latency means that a task takes longer to complete, which can result in slower performance and more complex workflows. In summary, throughput measures the capability to perform tasks efficiently, while latency measures the time it takes to complete tasks. High throughput and low latency are desirable in AI inference to achieve optimal performance and efficiency.
